summary: Implementation-only exact-match work for MN menu screen translation units. The PR reports 7 newly exact functions across 6 files, plus improvements to still-unmatched functions. Diff evidence shows mostly local C-shape, type-width, declaration-order, naming, inline-helper, ternary, and access-shape adjustments in menu files. Review specifically rejected dead literal constant-pool anchors in mndiagram.c, saying they should be handled during linking.

key_files.role: Largest menu character-select matching edits; changed local integer widths, temp names, pad access shape, declaration order, expression order, local temporaries, helper-call arguments, and a ternary slot count.
evidence_refs: changed_files: mncharsel.c added 84 deleted 76 hunks 25
raw_diff: mnCharSel_8025C020 int i/temp_r31 changed to u8 i/u8 hud_index
raw_diff: mnCharSel_CursorThink pad pointer replaced with HSD_PadCopyStatus[port] accesses
raw_diff: mnCharSel_80266D70_OnLeave if/else num_slots changed to ternary

key_files.role: Count menu match work including inline helper extraction around fn_802514D8.
evidence_refs: changed_files: mncount.c added 16 deleted 6 hunks 4

text_corpus issue_comment: fn_802514D8 became a new exact match

what_changed: Reported exact-match decompilation for MN menu screen translation units, with 7 newly exact functions across mnmain, mnname, mndiagram, mnsoundtest, mncount, and mncharsel.
Adjusted local variable types and names in mncharsel.c, including using u8 for character-select icon and HUD index paths where the diff previously used int/temp_r31.
Reshaped pad input reads in mnCharSel_CursorThink from an HSD_PadStatus pointer temporary to direct HSD_PadCopyStatus[port] field accesses.
Introduced or reordered temporaries and declarations in mncharsel.c for codegen, including jobj4/jobj43, tag_data, hdx_val/hdx, and reordered dx/dy/tx assignments.
Used a ternary expression for VS_CAMERA slot count in mnCharSel_80266D70_OnLeave, replacing an if/else.
Changed mncount.c around fn_802514D8 toward a static inline helper shape.
A reviewed mndiagram.c hunk attempted to add dead literal constant-pool anchors for .sdata2 layout; reviewer requested removal and said linking should fix it.

smart_moves.text: The PR kept the scope to one menu subsystem and listed all six touched MN files, which makes regression review and searchability easier.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Scope: MN menu screen translation units
human_pr_text: Files list includes six src/melee/mn files

smart_moves.text: Semantic local names were used where the role is visible in the diff, such as hud_index for ft_hudindex and target_count for gm_8016332C result, instead of preserving temp_rXX names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: temp_r31 renamed to hud_index
raw_diff: temp_r29 renamed to target_count

smart_moves.text: The diff used ordinary C forms in at least one local cleanup, replacing a small if/else assignment with a ternary for num_slots.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: num_slots = mnCharSel_804D6CB0->match_type == VS_CAMERA ? 3 : 4

decomp_lessons.lesson: For menu matching, small source-shape changes can matter: local type width, declaration order, field access form, temporary lifetime, expression order, and helper-call argument shape all appeared in the match-oriented diff.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: u8 i/u8 hud_index changes
raw_diff: declaration-order and temporary changes in fn_80262648 and mnCharSel_802640A0
raw_diff: HSD_PadCopyStatus direct access changes
human_pr_text: exact-match decompilation for MN menu screen translation units

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not rely on dead literal statements as constant-pool or .sdata2 anchors in reviewable matching source when reviewers indicate the layout should be fixed during linking.
evidence_refs: review_comments: Remove this, we will fix this during linking
review_comments hunk: (void) -1.0F and (void) 4503601774854144.0 dead literals reserved .sdata2 slots

decomp_lessons.lesson: Verification evidence in PR bodies should be cross-checked against bot reports when available; this slice body reports no regressions, while the loaded bot report also shows one unmatched .sdata2 regression in mndiagram.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Slice regression report: 7 new matches, 0 broken matches, 0 fuzzy regressions, 0 metric regressions
text_corpus issue_comment: 1 regression in an unmatched item: main/melee/mn/mndiagram .sdata2 91.67% to 90.91%

naming_conventions.name: hud_index
naming_conventions.convention: Use a semantic local name when a local value is clearly an ft_hudindex used across score/high-score lookups.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: int temp_r31 = icons[i].ft_hudindex changed to u8 hud_index = icons[i].ft_hudindex

naming_conventions.name: target_count
naming_conventions.convention: Use a role name for a Stadium Target count result instead of a temp_rXX local when the nearby formatting and sentinel check show the value's role.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: int temp_r29 = gm_8016332C(...) changed to int target_count = gm_8016332C(...)
raw_diff: target_count printed with %d or used to choose drawTimeText fallback

naming_conventions.name: tag_data
naming_conventions.convention: Introduce a local pointer with a role name when repeatedly accessing tp->data fields.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: CSSTagData* tag_data = tp->data; tag_data->state and tag_data->use_tag

naming_conventions.name: jobj4 / jobj43
naming_conventions.convention: Address- or joint-index-like local names may be retained for matching when semantics are weak but the local is known to be an HSD_JObj pointer.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_JObj* jobj4 used for lb_80011E24 joint 4
raw_diff: HSD_JObj* jobj43 used for lb_80011E24 joint 0x43

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use exact unsigned byte local types for byte-indexed menu fields when that is the matching shape.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: int i changed to u8 i for sel_icon
raw_diff: int temp_r31 changed to u8 hud_index for ft_hudindex
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Low to medium; type-width changes can affect casts and register extension behavior.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Direct array field access can replace a pointer temporary when matching pad input reads.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_PadStatus* pad removed; HSD_PadCopyStatus[port].stickY/stickX/trigger/button used directly
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Medium; access shape and load ordering may be codegen-sensitive.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Declaration order, local lifetime, and assignment grouping were used as narrow codegen controls.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_80262648 declaration order changes around jobj4, css, prev_port, iter/j
raw_diff: dx, tx, dy declared before assigning dx = model->x10 - (tx = model->x8)
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Medium; these are matching tactics and should be justified by objdiff/checkdiff evidence for the touched functions.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Inline helper extraction can close small remaining gaps.
evidence_refs: raw_diff excerpt: fn_802514D8 changed toward static inline fn_802514D8_inline
text_corpus issue_comment: fn_802514D8 became exact
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Medium; helper shape should not hide incorrect header ownership or prototype issues.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Avoid dead literal anchors for section layout when they are not real code; reviewer direction was to handle that during linking.
evidence_refs: review_comments: Remove this, we will fix this during linking
review_comments hunk: dead literals added solely to reserve .sdata2 slots
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: High if kept; can mix data/linking work into code matching and create noisy section regressions.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not use dead literal constant-pool anchors as code-match source
source_update_candidates.text: Reviewer evidence from PR #2689 supports clarifying that dead literal statements such as `(void) -1.0F;` added only to reserve .sdata2 or constant-pool slots should not remain in reviewable code-matching source; section layout should be handled through linking, symbol metadata, or data ownership fixes when appropriate.
evidence_refs: review_comments: Remove this, we will fix this during linking
review_comments hunk: dead literals used to reserve .sdata2 slots
source_update_candidates.reason: This is consistent with existing standards on literals/data ownership and data-section matching, but the curator may want to add the concrete constant-pool-anchor example.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR body's zero-regression statement as the only validation fact without noting the loaded bot report's unmatched .sdata2 regression.
Do not infer that every changed file used the same tactics as mncharsel.c; the loaded diff is truncated and detailed hunks for several files are unavailable.
Do not claim the constant-pool anchors were removed in the final merged diff; approval suggests review was resolved, but the loaded evidence does not show the final hunk state.
Do not generalize jobj4/jobj43 as preferred naming beyond this local matching context.
